parameters.query:
-- Approved cold-email drafts that are DUE. Step 1 sends immediately; later steps
-- send only once the previous step was sent AND the cadence gap has elapsed
-- (step2 = +3d, step3 = +2d, step4 = +3d after the previous step).
WITH gated AS (
  SELECT lo.id, lo.lead_id, lo.sequence_step, COALESCE(lo.approved_body, lo.draft_body) AS body,
         l.founder_email,
         p.sent_at AS prev_sent_at,
         CASE lo.sequence_step WHEN 2 THEN 3 WHEN 3 THEN 2 WHEN 4 THEN 3 ELSE 0 END AS offset_days
  FROM lead_outreach lo
  JOIN leads l ON l.id = lo.lead_id
  LEFT JOIN lead_outreach p ON p.lead_id = lo.lead_id AND p.channel='cold_email'
                            AND p.sequence_step = lo.sequence_step - 1 AND p.status='sent'
  WHERE lo.channel='cold_email' AND lo.status='approved' AND l.founder_email IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT id, lead_id, sequence_step, body, founder_email
FROM gated
WHERE sequence_step = 1
   OR (prev_sent_at IS NOT NULL AND NOW() >= prev_sent_at + (offset_days || ' days')::interval)
ORDER BY sequence_step, lead_id
LIMIT 50;

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ { to: $json.to, subject: $json.subject, body: $json.body, lead_id: $json.lead_id, outreach_id: $json.outreach_id, step: $json.step } }}

parameters.query: UPDATE lead_outreach SET status='sent', sent_at=NOW(), updated_at=NOW() WHERE id=$1 AND status='approved';

INSERT INTO outreach_events (lead_id, outreach_id, event_type, event_data, actor)
VALUES ($1, $2, 'send', $3::jsonb, 'system');

options.queryReplacement: ={{ [$('Split Subject/Body').item.json.lead_id, $('Split Subject/Body').item.json.outreach_id, JSON.stringify({ channel: 'cold_email', step: $('Split Subject/Body').item.json.step }) ] }}

parameters.content:
## Send Cold Email (automated — compliant)
Every 15 min: dispatches **approved** `cold_email` drafts that are DUE per the 4-touch cadence (step1 now; step2 +3d, step3 +2d, step4 +3d after the prior step is sent). Splits the folded `Subject:`/body, POSTs to the **existing 15-inbox stack** webhook (which owns inbox rotation + per-inbox caps + warmup), then marks sent + logs.

This is the fully-automated channel — no browser automation, no evasion. Email is a compliant B2B channel (respect CAN-SPAM / include opt-out in the template).

**Set:** `Postgres · warm_outreach` credential; env `COLD_EMAIL_SEND_WEBHOOK` (your existing stack's intake).
**Idempotent:** the `status='approved'` guard means a re-run never double-sends.
